content:
Hello,
Interacting with covenants is really frustrating and the quality of the game in this regard is not on par with other apsects of the game. I will try to explain why and offer suggestions.

**Use case**: I want to spend menophin to farm covenant in the Interlude game mode in order to create a new Covenant Plan and keep only the best pieces for the new plan. 

I go into the Interlude game mode and spend menophin to reenact a stage I have already played in order to get some covenant pieces.  After i spend it I can see the "Reenact Reward" screen. On this screen I can see the pieces I received but I have no option to dismantle them so they all accumulate into my inventory.
To dismantle them I have to quit the Interlude mode, go into my Inventory mode then covenants screen. I cannot dismantle them directly from this screen but instead I have to click one extra time on the small "dismantle" button at the bottom. That's already too many clicks from "getting covenant" to "managing the received covenant". 
__1st suggestion__: reduce UX friction by removing a needless click and allow covenants to be dismantled directly from the covenant page

Then comes the real issue: filtering covenants to dismantle them. The interface is really poor. As you can see on the screenshot the default view is very overloaded graphically, provides low quality information overall about my inventory, and the default sorting "Covenant Completion" text does not even fit within the box dedicated to sorting parameter name. I am playing on Steam on desktop. 
Now I would like to keep only the best pieces for my new covenant set. Most players would define "best" based on the substat level (max lvl is 8). If a piece has no substat over 6 I won't bother with it and would like to dismantle it. It is currently impossible to filter on substat level.
__2nd suggestion__: add a filter based on substat level so that all pieces with substat below a certain threshold get selected
So now I have to look at each piece one by one and click those with poor substat level to select them. Then I click dismantle. The pieces I have not selected I would like to lock them since I have considered they are good enough to keep for now. There is no way to mass lock covenant pieces. So I have to click on each and every one of them and then click the lock button.
__3rd suggestion__: allow for locking on multiple filtered pieces

So in summary I have had to click once or multiple times on each cov piece to either dismantle it or save it. That is way too many clicks and the user experience is really poor. If I have spent 1k menophin and some pieces have landed in my mailbox because of a full inventory I have to redo this process several times. 
The ideal solution is already implemented in other games: be able to sell/dismantle gear while you farm it based on some filters. So directly in the Interlude game mode I would be able to select "automatically dismantle covenant with all substats below X". This would save a lot of time and would increase the quality of this game aspect quite considerably. At the very least the 3 suggestions listed above would go a long way.

Thanks!
<@&1166257943742787615> for your consideration. I hope I was clear enough and I really hope this aspect of the game can be improved moving forward. Have a great day!

One of the main stress points with the new ranking reward system is the weighting of rewards towards the top rank, which is limited to a fraction of a minority of the playerbase. This is because the ranking tiers are logarithmic. To take P-Dive as an example, we see that:
```
1st rank: Top 0.2%
2nd rank: 0.2% to 1%
3rd rank: 1% to 5%
4th rank: 5% to 15%
5th rank: 15% to 35%
6th rank: 35% to 75%
7th rank: 75% to 100%
```
However, the rewards granted per rank are linear. The rewards for each rank are:
```
1st rank: 50 dreamsparks - Top 0.2% of players
2nd rank: 40 dreamsparks - 0.2% to 1%
3rd rank: 30 dreamsparks - 1% to 5%
4th rank: 20 dreamsparks - 5% to 15%
5th rank: 10 dreamsparks - 15% to 35%
6th rank: 5 dreamsparks - 35% to 75%
7th rank: 0 dreamsparks - 75% to 100%
```
Since the top ranking values are so incredibly small (top 0.2% and top 1% of players), the significantly increased reward amount being given to these ranks becomes unreachable for most players. Very few players will ever be even in the top 5% of the playerbase, much less top 1% and top 0.2%. The disparity between high rankers and the average player means players who are not within the top 5% of the playerbase barely receive any rewards, which can be demotivating and prevent the vast majority of the playerbase from engaging with or caring about the content.

As an example, if we take a top player in the top 0.2% of ranking, they will receive 50 dreamsparks. Comparatively, an "average" player, who would be right at the 50% baseline of the playerbase, will get the 6th rank, or 5 dreamsparks. This means that a top player earns __**10 times**__ the rewards that an average player receives. Even a fairly above average player, in the top 20-25%, would only receive 10, meaning that a top player still receives __**5 times**__ as many rewards as even a fairly high-level player.
thread_title: Normalize ranking rewards to encourage participation

content:
Additionally, the focus on mandatory ranking in order to receive decent rewards causes an atmosphere of competition and stress. While some players enjoy this, I believe that the majority of players who play the game for PvE content do not enjoy competition and fighting amongst the playerbase. By promoting competition for the sake of rewards, there will be an increased chance of burnout and more stress for less motivated casual players who will likely prefer to disengage from the game and ranking systems rather than fight for a higher ranking.

I would like to suggest normalizing the rewards to provide a higher baseline reward level, which will incentivize players to enjoy a decent participation reward, while lowering the disparity in ranking rewards between top-level players and the majority of the playerbase. For example, a more normalized distribution may look something like:
```
1st rank: 50 dreamsparks
2nd rank: 45 dreamsparks
3rd rank: 40 dreamsparks
4th rank: 35 dreamsparks
5th rank: 30 dreamsparks
6th rank: 25 dreamsparks
7th rank: 20 dreamsparks
```
With this model, a top 0.2% player would only receive twice as many rewards as the average player (50%), and only 1.5x as many rewards as a high level player (20-25%). I believe this model would reduce the dissatisfaction that normal players feel when seeing a minority of top rankers enjoy rewards which are many times higher.
thread_title: Normalize ranking rewards to encourage participation

content:
In fact, to suggest an even more aggressive curve, it may be advisable to focus more on cosmetic rewards rather than character growth and progression rewards for the highest level. In this case, perhaps something like this could be possible:
```
1st rank: 50 dreamsparks + 30 formless
2nd rank: 50 dreamsparks + 20 formless
3rd rank: 50 dreamsparks + 10 formless
4th rank: 45 dreamsparks
5th rank: 40 dreamsparks
6th rank: 35 dreamsparks
7th rank: 30 dreamsparks
```
This model would allow players to get the full amount of growth and progression materials as long as they are in the top 5% of the playerbase, while allowing the average player in the 50% range to still receive most of the growth rewards available to assist with their account progression. This would reduce the disparity between lower-level players and top-end players to around 1.5x, which I believe is a reasonable range with regards to growth and progression materials.
thread_title: Normalize ranking rewards to encourage participation
